text: Weighting Estimation Panel Data Non-Response
code: library(devtools)                               # Load package containing install_github install_github("statisticsnorway/ssb-calibratessb") # Install CalibrateSSB from GitHub library(CalibrateSSB)                           # Load CalibrateSSB ?CalibrateSSB                                   # Help documentation of function CalibrateSSB ?PanelEstimation                                # Help documentation of function PanelEstimation

title: Weighting and Estimation for Panel Data with Non-Response
text: CalibrateSSB R-package handles repeated surveys partially overlapping samples. Initially samples weighted linear calibration using known estimated population totals. robust model based covariance matrix relevant estimated totals calculated residuals according calibration model. Alternatively design based covariance matrix calculated similar way. cluster robust version also possible. case estimated populations totals covariance matrix adjusted utilizing theory Särndal Lundström (2005). Variances linear combinations (changes averages) ratios calculated covariance matrix. linear combinations ratios can involve variables within /sample waves.

title: Weighting and Estimation for Panel Data with Non-Response
text: Langsrud, Ø (2016): “variance estimation R-package repeated surveys - useful estimates changes quarterly annual averages”, Romanian Statistical Review nr. 2 / 2016, pp. 17-28. CONFERENCE: New Challenges Statistical Software - Use R Official Statistics, Bucharest, Romania, 7-8 April. Särndal, C.-E. Lundström, S. (2005): Estimation Surveys Nonresponse, John Wiley Sons, New York.

title: Create or modify a CalSSB object — CalSSBobj
text: CalSSB object. , object type retuned CalibrateSSB.

title: Create or modify a CalSSB object — CalSSBobj
text: x ReGenesees/cal.analytic object, function wrapper CalSSBobjReGenesees.

title: Create or modify a CalSSB object — CalSSBobj
code: #' # Generates data - two years z <- AkuData(3000)  # 3000 in each quarter zPop <- AkuData(10000)[, 1:7]  # Create a CalSSB object by CalibrateSSB b <- CalibrateSSB(z, calmodel = "~ sex*age", partition = c("year", "q"), popData = zPop,                    y = c("unemployed", "workforce"))  # Modify the CalSSB object a <- CalSSBobj(b, w = 10*b$w, wave = CrossStrata(z[, c("year", "q")]), id = z$id)  # Use the CalSSB object as input ... PanelEstimation(WideFromCalibrate(a), "unemployed", linComb = PeriodDiff(8, 4)) #> $wTot #> 2014-1 2014-2 2014-3 2014-4 2015-1 2015-2 2015-3 2015-4  #>  1e+05  1e+05  1e+05  1e+05  1e+05  1e+05  1e+05  1e+05  #>  #> $estimates #> unemployed-2014-1 unemployed-2014-2 unemployed-2014-3 unemployed-2014-4  #>          2034.624          2158.891          1595.673          2066.855  #> unemployed-2015-1 unemployed-2015-2 unemployed-2015-3 unemployed-2015-4  #>          1696.414          2191.972          1900.522          1853.707  #>  #> $linCombs #> [1] -53.35698 #>  #> $varEstimates #>   2014-1   2014-2   2014-3   2014-4   2015-1   2015-2   2015-3   2015-4  #> 73910.73 79509.11 61042.94 81125.75 67323.78 87301.27 78545.71 78167.28  #>  #> $varLinCombs #> [1] 46304.5 #>   # Create CalSSB object without x as input CalSSBobj(y = b$y, w = 10*b$w, resids = b$resids, wave = CrossStrata(z[, c("year", "q")]),            id = z$id) #> ---- n = 24000     ny = 2  #>  #> ---- y names ---- #>  unemployed workforce  #>  #> ---- Calibrated weights, w ---- #>  38.48901 38.48901 38.18021 38.18021 38.18021 38.58311 ... 44.0604  #>  #> ---- Summary of calSSB object ---- #>        Length Class      Mode    #> y          2  data.frame list    #> w      24000  -none-     numeric #> resids     2  data.frame list    #> id     24000  -none-     numeric #> wave   24000  factor     numeric

title: Create a CalSSB object from a ReGenesees/cal.analytic object — CalSSBobjReGenesees
text: x Output ReGenesees::e.calibrate() (object class cal.analytic) y formula variable names samplingWeights NULL, TRUE (capture x), formula, variable name vector data extra NULL, formula, variable names matrix data id NULL, TRUE (ids x), formula, variable name vector data wave NULL,  formula, variable name vector data

title: Create a CalSSB object from a ReGenesees/cal.analytic object — CalSSBobjReGenesees
text: CalSSB object. , object type retuned CalibrateSSB.

title: Create a CalSSB object from a ReGenesees/cal.analytic object — CalSSBobjReGenesees
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Generates data - two years z <- AkuData(3000)  # 3000 in each quarter zPop <- AkuData(10000)[, 1:7] z$samplingWeights <- 1 z$ids <- 1:NROW(z)  # Create a ReGenesees/cal.analytic object library("ReGenesees") desReGenesees <- e.svydesign(z[z$R == 1, ], ids = ~ids, weights = ~samplingWeights) popTemplate <- pop.template(data = desReGenesees, calmodel = ~sex * age, partition = ~year + q) popTotals <- fill.template(universe = zPop, template = popTemplate) calReGenesees <- e.calibrate(design = desReGenesees, df.population = popTotals)  # Create CalSSB objects from a ReGenesees/cal.analytic object CalSSBobjReGenesees(calReGenesees, y = ~unemployed + workforce, id = TRUE,                      samplingWeights = TRUE, extra = ~famid) a <- CalSSBobjReGenesees(calReGenesees, y = c("unemployed", "workforce"),                           id = "id", extra = "famid", wave = c("year", "q"))  # Use the CalSSB object as input ... PanelEstimation(WideFromCalibrate(a), "unemployed", linComb = PeriodDiff(8, 4))  } # }

text: CalibrateSSB R-package handles repeated surveys partially overlapping samples. Initially samples weighted linear calibration using known estimated population totals. robust model based covariance matrix relevant estimated totals calculated residuals according calibration model. Alternatively design based covariance matrix calculated similar way. cluster robust version also possible. case estimated populations totals covariance matrix adjusted utilizing theory Särndal Lundström (2005). Variances linear combinations (changes averages) ratios calculated covariance matrix. linear combinations ratios can involve variables within /sample waves.

title: Calibration weighting and estimation — CalibrateSSB
text: Compute weights calibration corresponding estimates, totals residuals

title: Calibration weighting and estimation — CalibrateSSB
code: CalibrateSSB(   grossSample,   calmodel = NULL,   response = "R",   popTotals = NULL,   y = NULL,   by = NULL,   partition = NULL,   lRegmodel = NULL,   popData = NULL,   samplingWeights = NULL,   usePackage = "survey",   bounds = c(-Inf, Inf),   calfun = "linear",   onlyTotals = FALSE,   onlyw = FALSE,   uselRegWeights = FALSE,   ids = NULL,   residOutput = TRUE,   leverageOutput = FALSE,   yOutput = TRUE,   samplingWeightsOutput = FALSE,   dropResid2 = TRUE,   wGrossOutput = TRUE,   wave = NULL,   id = NULL,   extra = NULL,   allowNApopTotals = NULL,   partitionPrint = NULL,   ginvtol = 1e-06,   boundstol = 1e-06,   resids_by_lm = FALSE,   ... )

title: Calibration weighting and estimation — CalibrateSSB
text: grossSample Input data, typically data frame, tibble, data.table. grossSample classic data frame, coerced one internally. calmodel Formula defining linear structure calibration model. response Variable name response indicator (net sample 1). popTotals Population totals (similar population totals output). popTotals classic data frame, coerced one internally. y Names variables interest. Can list similar "" . Names variables define "estimation domains". NULL (default option) NA estimates refer whole population. Use list multiple specifications (resulting list output). partition Names variables define "calibration domains" model. NULL (default) implies calibration domains. lRegmodel Formula defining linear structure logistic regression model. popData Population data, typically data frame, tibble, data.table. popData classic data frame, coerced one internally. samplingWeights Name variable initial weights sampling units. usePackage Specifying package used: "survey" (default), "ReGenesees" "none". bounds Bounds calibration weights. ReGenesees: Allowed range ratios calibrated initial weights. default c(-Inf,Inf). calfun distance function calibration process; default 'linear'. onlyTotals TRUE: population totals returned. onlyw TRUE: calibrated weights returned. uselRegWeights TRUE: Weighted logistic regression performed first calibration step. ids Name sampling unit identifier variable. residOutput Residuals output TRUE. FALSE default. leverageOutput Leverages output TRUE. FALSE default. yOutput y output TRUE. FALSE default. samplingWeightsOutput samplingWeights output TRUE. FALSE default. dropResid2 TRUE (default) missing population totals one set residuals output. wGrossOutput wGross output TRUE (default) NA popTotals. wave Time another repeat variable (included output). id Identifier variable (included output). extra Variables extra dataset (included output). allowNApopTotals TRUE missing population totals allowed. Results error FALSE warning NULL. partitionPrint TRUE partition progress printed. Automatic decision NULL (1 min total computing time). ginvtol Tolerance parameter generalized inverse used usePackage = "none" also computation resids resids_by_lm = FALSE leverageOutput = FALSE. default (1e-06) chosen robustness function mind, beyond machine precision effects. standard value 1.5e-08, similar ginv() MASS package. boundstol Tolerance parameter used iterations involving bounds usePackage = "none". resids_by_lm FALSE (default) leverageOutput = FALSE, computation resids based generalized inverse. Otherwise, computations go via lm() function. ... arguments sent underlying functions.

title: Calibration weighting and estimation — CalibrateSSB
text: Unless onlyTotals onlyw TRUE, output object class calSSB. , list elements: popTotals Population totals. w calibrated weights. wGross Calibrated gross sample weights NA popTotals. estTM Estimates (standard error). resids Residuals, reduced model NA popTotals. resids2 Residuals, full model. leverages Diagonal elements hat-matrix, reduced model NA popTotals. leverages2 Diagonal elements hat-matrix, full model. y input samplingWeights input wave input via CrossStrata id input extra input

title: Calibration weighting and estimation — CalibrateSSB
text: popTotals input NULL, population totals computed popData (available) grossSample. elements popTotals may missing (allowed using ReGenesees). using "ReGenesees", weiging estimation done package. using "survey", calibration weiging done package. parameters wave, id extra effect computations, result extra elements output (used WideFromCalibrate() later).

title: Calibration weighting and estimation — CalibrateSSB
code: # Generates data  - two years z    <- AkuData(3000)  # 3000 in each quarter zPop <- AkuData(10000)[,1:7]  # Calibration using "survey" a <- CalibrateSSB(z, calmodel = "~ sex*age",                  partition = c("year","q"),  # calibrate within quarter                  popData = zPop, y = c("unemployed","workforce"),                  by = c("year","q")) # Estimate within quarter head(a$w) # calibrated weights #> [1] 4.202268 3.387097 3.387097 0.000000 3.387097 4.029891 a$estTM   # estimates #>   year q unemployed workforce #> 1 2014 1   255.1660  7268.272 #> 3 2014 2   271.8313  7333.530 #> 5 2014 3   225.0274  7325.043 #> 7 2014 4   177.0589  7346.346 #> 2 2015 1   199.6609  7296.701 #> 4 2015 2   166.6121  7281.029 #> 6 2015 3   185.7072  7343.383 #> 8 2015 4   167.9851  7288.499 a$popTotals   # popTotals used as input below #>   year q (Intercept) sex1 age2 age3 sex1:age2 sex1:age3 #> 1 2014 1       10000 5059 4424 2718      2223      1353 #> 2 2014 2       10000 5046 4424 2614      2226      1299 #> 3 2014 3       10000 5057 4376 2622      2214      1306 #> 4 2014 4       10000 5082 4394 2557      2257      1277 #> 5 2015 1       10000 5088 4338 2684      2248      1358 #> 6 2015 2       10000 5093 4264 2724      2201      1380 #> 7 2015 3       10000 5081 4285 2638      2210      1318 #> 8 2015 4       10000 5072 4204 2629      2147      1338   # Calibration, no package, popTotals as input b <- CalibrateSSB(z, popTotals=a$popTotals, calmodel="~ sex*age",       partition = c("year","q"), usePackage = "none", y = c("unemployed","workforce")) max(abs(a$w-b$w)) # Same weights as above #> [1] 2.096101e-13  print(a) #> ---- estTM ---- #>   year q unemployed workforce #> 1 2014 1   255.1660  7268.272 #> 3 2014 2   271.8313  7333.530 #> 5 2014 3   225.0274  7325.043 #> 7 2014 4   177.0589  7346.346 #> 2 2015 1   199.6609  7296.701 #> 4 2015 2   166.6121  7281.029 #> 6 2015 3   185.7072  7343.383 #> 8 2015 4   167.9851  7288.499 #>  #>  #> ---- n = 24000     ny = 2  #>  #> ---- y names ---- #>  unemployed workforce  #>  #> ---- Calibrated weights, w ---- #>  4.202268 3.387097 3.387097 0 3.387097 4.029891 ... 4.54023  #>  #> ---- Summary of calSSB object ---- #>           Length Class      Mode    #> popTotals     8  data.frame list    #> w         24000  -none-     numeric #> estTM         4  data.frame list    #> resids        2  data.frame list    #> y             2  data.frame list    print(b) #> ---- estTM ---- #>   unemployed workforce #> 1   1649.049   58482.8 #>  #>  #> ---- n = 24000     ny = 2  #>  #> ---- y names ---- #>  unemployed workforce  #>  #> ---- Calibrated weights, w ---- #>  4.202268 3.387097 3.387097 0 3.387097 4.029891 ... 4.54023  #>  #> ---- Summary of calSSB object ---- #>           Length Class      Mode    #> popTotals     8  data.frame list    #> w         24000  -none-     numeric #> estTM         2  data.frame list    #> resids        2  data.frame list    #> y             2  data.frame list     if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ require(ReGenesees) # Calibration and estimation via ReGenesees CalibrateSSB(z, calmodel = "~ sex*age",              partition = c("year","q"),  # calibrate within quarter              popData = zPop, usePackage = "ReGenesees",              y = c("unemployed","workforce"),              by = c("year","q")) # Estimate within quarter } # }

title: Calibration weighting and variance estimation for panel data — CalibrateSSBpanel
code: z    = AkuData(3000)  # 3000 in each quarter zPop = AkuData(10000)[,1:7] lc = rbind(LagDiff(8,4),PeriodDiff(8,4)) rownames(lc) = c("diffQ1","diffQ2","diffQ3","diffQ4","diffYearMean") CalibrateSSBpanel(grossSample=z,calmodel="~ sex*age", partition=c("year","q"),popData=zPop,         y=c("unemployed","workforce"),id="id",wave=c("year","q"),        numerator="unemployed",linComb=lc) #> $wTot #> 2014-1 2014-2 2014-3 2014-4 2015-1 2015-2 2015-3 2015-4  #>  10000  10000  10000  10000  10000  10000  10000  10000  #>  #> $estimates #> unemployed-2014-1 unemployed-2014-2 unemployed-2014-3 unemployed-2014-4  #>          173.9406          218.1464          197.3393          154.4800  #> unemployed-2015-1 unemployed-2015-2 unemployed-2015-3 unemployed-2015-4  #>          258.1992          171.8048          222.0931          217.4283  #>  #> $linCombs #>       diffQ1       diffQ2       diffQ3       diffQ4 diffYearMean  #>     84.25857    -46.34153     24.75383     62.94829     31.40479  #>  #> $varEstimates #>   2014-1   2014-2   2014-3   2014-4   2015-1   2015-2   2015-3   2015-4  #> 485.3383 645.7710 608.5104 513.1668 825.1369 554.6611 706.3391 725.3746  #>  #> $varLinCombs #>       diffQ1       diffQ2       diffQ3       diffQ4 diffYearMean  #>    1324.7488    1105.0741    1308.0700    1186.2428     366.5035  #>

text: Dataframe matrix several variables sep Used create new level names returnb TRUE overview original variabels according new levels also retuned. asNumeric TRUE new variable numeric. byExtra Contains variables represents another data set.

text: new variable aExtra New variable according byExtra b Overview original variabels according new levels

title: Creation of linear combination matrices — LinCombMatrix
code: LinCombMatrix(   n,   period = NULL,   lag = NULL,   k = 0,   takeMean = TRUE,   removerows = TRUE,   overlap = FALSE )  LagDiff(n, lag = 1, removerows = TRUE)  Period(   n,   period = 1,   k = 0,   takeMean = TRUE,   removerows = TRUE,   overlap = FALSE )  PeriodDiff(   n,   period = 1,   lag = period,   k = 0,   takeMean = TRUE,   removerows = TRUE,   overlap = FALSE )

what: Variance estimation for panel data — PanelEstimation
title: Variance estimation for panel data — PanelEstimation
text: Variance estimation linear combinations totals ratios based output wideFromCalibrate

title: Variance estimation for panel data — PanelEstimation
code: PanelEstimation(   x,   numerator,   denominator = NULL,   linComb = matrix(0, 0, n),   linComb0 = NULL,   estType = "robustModel",   leveragePower = 1/2,   group = NULL,   returnCov = FALSE,   usewGross = TRUE )

title: Variance estimation for panel data — PanelEstimation
text: x Output wideFromCalibrate. numerator y variable name number. denominator y variable name number. linComb Matrix defining linear combinations waves. linComb0 Linear combination matrix used prior ratio calculations. estType Estimation type: "robustModel" (default), "ssbAKU", "robustModelww", "robustModelGroup" "robustModelGroupww" (see ) leveragePower Power used adjusting residuals using leverages. group Extra variable name number cluster robust estimation. returnCov Return covariance matrices instead variance vectors. usewGross Use wGross (avaliable) instead design weights adjust covariance matrix case NA popTotals

title: Variance estimation for panel data — PanelEstimation
text: wTot Sum weights estimates Ordinary estimates linCombs Estimates linear combinations varEstimates Variance estimates varLinCombs Variance estimates linear combinations denominator specified output refer ratios. , similar output numerator denominator also included.

title: Variance estimation for panel data — PanelEstimation
text: denominator=NULL, estimates single y-variable (numerator) calculated. denominator specified, estimates numerator, denominator ratio calculated. default estimation type parameter, "robustModel", equation (12) paper. "ssbAKU" (16), "robustModelww" (9) "robustModelGroup" "robustModelGroupww" cluster robust variants based \((w-1)^2\) \(w^2\) .

title: Variance estimation for panel data — PanelEstimation
code: # Generates data  - two years z    = AkuData(3000)  # 3000 in each quarter zPop = AkuData(10000)[,1:7]  # Calibration and "WideFromCalibrate" b = CalibrateSSB(z,calmodel="~ sex*age", partition=c("year","q"),         popData=zPop, y=c("unemployed","workforce")) bWide = WideFromCalibrate(b,CrossStrata(z[,c("year","q")]),z$id)  # Define linear combination matrix lc = rbind(LagDiff(8,4),PeriodDiff(8,4)) rownames(lc) = c("diffQ1","diffQ2","diffQ3","diffQ4","diffYearMean") colnames(lc) = colnames(head(bWide$y[[1]])) lc #>              2014-1 2014-2 2014-3 2014-4 2015-1 2015-2 2015-3 2015-4 #> diffQ1        -1.00   0.00   0.00   0.00   1.00   0.00   0.00   0.00 #> diffQ2         0.00  -1.00   0.00   0.00   0.00   1.00   0.00   0.00 #> diffQ3         0.00   0.00  -1.00   0.00   0.00   0.00   1.00   0.00 #> diffQ4         0.00   0.00   0.00  -1.00   0.00   0.00   0.00   1.00 #> diffYearMean  -0.25  -0.25  -0.25  -0.25   0.25   0.25   0.25   0.25  # Unemployed: Totals and linear combinations d1=PanelEstimation(bWide,"unemployed",linComb=lc)  #  # Table of output cbind(tot=d1$estimates,se=sqrt(d1$varEstimates)) #>                        tot       se #> unemployed-2014-1 203.2301 23.37956 #> unemployed-2014-2 207.3415 24.22232 #> unemployed-2014-3 136.7243 20.14655 #> unemployed-2014-4 171.9668 23.18518 #> unemployed-2015-1 230.9932 27.03446 #> unemployed-2015-2 187.6141 24.00314 #> unemployed-2015-3 162.6746 22.06865 #> unemployed-2015-4 217.8148 26.50928 cbind(tot=d1$linCombs,se=sqrt(d1$varLinCombs)) #>                    tot       se #> diffQ1        27.76308 33.62251 #> diffQ2       -19.72731 33.93508 #> diffQ3        25.95028 27.83716 #> diffQ4        45.84805 34.17877 #> diffYearMean  19.95852 18.45563  # Ratio: Totals and linear combinations d=PanelEstimation(bWide,numerator="unemployed",denominator="workforce",linComb=lc) cbind(tot=d$estimates,se=sqrt(d$varEstimates)) #>               tot          se #> 2014-1 0.02799162 0.003206968 #> 2014-2 0.02849977 0.003314161 #> 2014-3 0.01901122 0.002794352 #> 2014-4 0.02441536 0.003281478 #> 2015-1 0.03174747 0.003700882 #> 2015-2 0.02538527 0.003237273 #> 2015-3 0.02242822 0.003032731 #> 2015-4 0.02994036 0.003629313 cbind(tot=d$linCombs,se=sqrt(d$varLinCombs)) #>                       tot          se #> diffQ1        0.003755844 0.004592525 #> diffQ2       -0.003114504 0.004596087 #> diffQ3        0.003416997 0.003836811 #> diffQ4        0.005524996 0.004727154 #> diffYearMean  0.002395833 0.002546163  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Calibration when som population totals unknown (edu) # Leverages in output (will be used to adjust residuals) # Cluster robust estimation (families/famid) b2 = CalibrateSSB(z,popData=zPop,calmodel="~ edu*sex + sex*age",            partition=c("year","q"), y=c("unemployed","workforce"),            leverageOutput=TRUE) b2Wide = WideFromCalibrate(b2,CrossStrata(z[,c("year","q")]),z$id,extra=z$famid) d2 = PanelEstimation(b2Wide,"unemployed",linComb=lc,group=1,estType = "robustModelGroup") cbind(tot=d2$linCombs,se=sqrt(d2$varLinCombs)) } # }   # Yearly mean before ratio calculation (linComb0) # and difference between years (linComb) g=PanelEstimation(bWide,numerator="unemployed",denominator="workforce",     linComb= LagDiff(2),linComb0=Period(8,4)) cbind(tot=g$linCombs,se=sqrt(g$varLinCombs)) #>              tot          se #> [1,] 0.002371494 0.002548562

title: CalibrateSSB 1.4.0
text: package now documentation site https://statisticsnorway.github.io/ssb-calibratessb/. Control robustness precision regression computations. Internal coercion data.frame using .data.frame() necessary. Correct output sampling-weight data instead samplingWeights variable name. Avoidance downstream errors WideFromCalibrate() CalibrateSSBpanel().
